    Magnetic Fields in Star Formation

    B-Fields in Star Formation

    Type:Intermediate concept
    Typical strengths:10–1000 μG (cloud to core scales)
    Key diagnostic:Mass-to-flux ratio λ
    Related: Magnetic fields
    Star formation
    Dust polarimetry

    Magnetic fields play a central role in regulating the star formation process, from controlling the fragmentation of molecular clouds to launching protostellar outflows. The key question is whether magnetic fields are strong enough to significantly delay or regulate gravitational collapse, or whether they are subdominant to turbulence and gravity.

    B-Field Orientation Relative to Filaments

    Planck and Herschel observations reveal a systematic relationship between B-field orientation and gas density in filamentary clouds: magnetic fields are predominantly perpendicular to high-column-density filaments (N(H₂) > 5×10²¹ cm⁻²) but parallel to low-density striations[1][2]. This transition is reproduced in MHD simulations and reflects the dynamical importance of fields: in the strong-field regime, matter flows preferentially along field lines, forming filaments perpendicular to the field[3].

    Mass-to-Flux Ratio and Magnetic Criticality

    The mass-to-flux ratio λ quantifies whether a cloud is magnetically subcritical (λ < 1, B-field prevents collapse), supercritical (λ > 1, gravity dominates), or transcritical (λ ~ 1). In the IRDC G351.77-0.53, λ ~ 0.9 ± 0.6 on cloud scales transitions to λ > 1 near the densest clump c1, consistent with magnetically regulated collapse[4].

    Hourglass Morphology as Collapse Signature

    When a magnetized cloud core contracts under gravity, initially uniform field lines are dragged inward, producing a characteristic hourglass-shaped B-field morphology[5]. This has been observed in several star-forming regions including OMC-1 (Orion)[6], DR21[7], and G351.77-0.53[4]. The hourglass is a smoking-gun signature of magnetically regulated gravitational collapse.

    Energy Budget

    In star-forming clouds, the relative contributions of magnetic (E_B = B²V/8π), gravitational (E_G = GM²/L), and kinetic (E_K = Mσ_V²) energies determine the dynamical state. In G351.77-0.53, all three energies are comparable (~10⁴⁷ ergs), confirming that B-fields are dynamically important[4][8].

    B-Field Studies in IRDCs

    Magnetic field observations in IRDCs — the earliest stages of massive star formation — remain relatively rare but are growing. Key results include:

    • G11.11-0.12 — SOFIA/HAWC+ reveals B-field structure influencing star formation[9]
    • G14.225-0.506 — magnetically dominated with parallel inter-filament fields[10]
    • G28.37+0.07 — complex B-field controlling fragmentation[11]
    • G351.77-0.53 — hourglass morphology and transcritical magnetization[4]

    See Also

    References
    1. Planck Collaboration (2016). "B-fields in filamentary structures." A&A, 586, A138. DOI:10.1051/0004-6361/201525896
    2. Soler, J. D., et al. (2017). "B-field orientation transition." A&A, 603, A64. DOI:10.1051/0004-6361/201731049
    3. Soler, J. D., et al. (2013). "Dust polarization morphology and cloud magnetization." ApJ, 774, 128. DOI:10.1088/0004-637X/774/2/128
    4. Jadhav, O. R., et al. (2025). "Hourglass B-field toward IRDC G351.77-0.53." arXiv:2603.16425
    5. Mouschovias, T. Ch. (1976). "Magnetic field support." ApJ, 207, 141. DOI:10.1086/154835
    6. Chuss, D. T., et al. (2019). "SOFIA/HAWC+ multiwavelength polarimetry of OMC-1." ApJ, 872, 187. DOI:10.3847/1538-4357/aafd37
    7. Ching, T.-C., et al. (2022). "DR21 JCMT BISTRO-2." ApJ, 941, 122. DOI:10.3847/1538-4357/ac9dfb
    8. Fiege, J. D. & Pudritz, R. E. (2000). "Helical Fields and Filamentary Clouds." ApJ, 534, 291. DOI:10.1086/317228
    9. Ngoc, N. B., et al. (2023). "SOFIA/HAWC+ of G11.11-0.12." ApJ, 953, 66. DOI:10.3847/1538-4357/acdb6e
    10. Santos, F. P., et al. (2016). "B-fields in G14.225-0.506." ApJ, 832, 186. DOI:10.3847/0004-637X/832/2/186
    11. Law, C. Y., et al. (2024). "B-fields in G28.37+0.07." ApJ, 966, 116. DOI:10.3847/1538-4357/ad39e0
